title: Extraction of high molecular weight DNA from bacteriophage
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4pprv","text":"Extraction of High Molecular Weight DNA from dsDNA bacteriophages (phage) can be achieved using an isopropanol precipitation method which can be used for long read whole genome sequencing, such as Oxford Nanopore Technology.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9hlo4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dh2k4","text":"This is a forked protocol from https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3byl4qwqovo5/v1, which instead uses zinc chloride for bacteriophage virion precipitation to concentrate the sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":31,"length":56}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"16584C9B5A1F11EFAE7D0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3byl4qwqovo5/v1"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ae3aj","text":"Version 3: fixed a bug where protocols.io had selected \"Mass Percent\" as default instead of the originally selected \"Molarity (M)\" unit. EDTA and zinc chloride steps now show reagent units correctly as Molarity (M).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epn2n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90ooh","text":"General: This protocol can be used to extract DNA from bacteriophage within bacterial lysates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8m8na","text":"It is ideal to extract phages which have a titre of 1 x 1010 to 1 x 1011 PFU/mL, however lower titres can be extracted successfully.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":58,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":70,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5gfm8","text":"This protocol extracts DNA from a 700μL aliquot of bacteriophage lysate, however the starting lysate volume can be scaled up. If scaling up, adjust the reagent volumes in all steps up until the zinc chloride pelleting step, ensuring consistent ratios throughout the protocol to this point. Once the zinc chloride pellet has had the supernatant removed, the protocol volumes resume at the described amount.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c15av","text":"\n\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2ep0","text":"While this protocol does not utilise common hazardous chemicals used for DNA extraction (phenol/chloroform), care should be taken to follow the recommendations in the MSDS provided which each reagent, and ensure proper storage for the dangerous goods utilised in this protocol (flammable liquids etc)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"529rs","text":"The following materials were utilised for this protocol:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"93lmu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1d103","text":"Puregene tissue kit (Qiagen, #158023) containing cell lysis solution, protein precipitation buffer, proteinase K, RNase A (4 mg/mL), and DNA hydration buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71bng","text":"Ethanol, molecular grade (Sigma, #E7023)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hgrh","text":"UltraPure molecular grade water (ThermoFisher, #10977015)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tt5r","text":"DNAse I (NEB, #M0303S)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2itt2","text":"DNAse I 10x reaction buffer (NEB, #B0303SVIAL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2gdqi","text":"Isopropanol, molecular grade (Sigma, #I9516)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6v7i7","text":"Zinc chloride (ZnCl2) powder (Merck, Cat#: Z0152-50G)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":19,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"179mu","text":"Hydrochloric acid 32% AR Grade (Chem Supply, Cat# HA020-500M)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"403u2","text":"DNA LoBind 2.0mL tubes (Thermo Fisher, #30108.078)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Ethanol (70% v/v) Create a 70 % (v/v) solution of molecular grade ethanol using UltraPure water (approx. 400 µL per sample)
Add 78 µL of Digestion Master Mix to 700 µL of syringe filtered ( 0.22 µm ) bacteriophage lysate in a 1.5mL LoBind microtube and mix via gentle pipetting

Incubate for 00:30:00 at 37 °C using a Thermomixer without shaking Note: longer incubation times may be necessary to achieve complete degradation of bacterial DNA and RNA. It is advisable to quantify the amount of bacterial DNA and RNA pre- and post-digestion to ensure bacterial nucleic acids do not contaminate phage DNA. Alternatively, increase the volume of DNase I and RNase A within the Digestion Master Mix as discussed above. Assessing the DNA quality of both untreated lysate and treated lysate using agarose gel electrophoresis can be useful to determine the host nucleic acid sizes in your samples, and whether a post-extraction SPRI bead clean up may be required (described elsewhere).
Add 2.5 % (v/v) of 2 Molarity (M) zinc chloride solution (freshly made) to the bacteriophage lysate for a final concentration of 50 millimolar (mM) and mix via gentle pipetting
Add 1.5 µL of Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) and 250 µL of Cell Lysis Solution to the phage pellet and mix by gently pipetting up and down 25 times
Incubate the sample for 00:05:00 at 37 °C

Digestion Master Mix Calculate the required volume of Digestion Mix for the number of samples you wish to process Each 700 µL sample of bacteriophage lysate requires 78 µL of Digestion Mix Create this by adding 1 µL, (up to 5 uL*) of DNAse I and 1 µL, (up to 5 uL*) of RNAse A to 76 µL, (adjust to 78 uL final volume) of 10X DNAse I reaction buffer and place on ice. The amount of each enzyme can be increased if the lysate has high levels of host DNA or RNA. We recommend quantifying the amount of host nucleic acid in your sample both pre- and post-digestion to understand your specific requirements. Our group has used 5 uL of each enzyme with favourable results. Quantify the nucleic acid within untreated and treated samples using a highly sensitive method such as the QuBit HS assay kit(s).
Place sample on ice for 00:01:00
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 56 °C in a thermomixer at 500 rpm, °C, and then pre-cool a centrifuge to 4 °C
Add 100 µL of protein precipitation solution and mix thoroughly by inverting the tube 50 times
Incubate samples on ice for 00:05:00
Centrifuge tube at 16000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Add supernatant to a new 1.5mL LoBind tube, careful not to disturb the protein pellet
Add 300 µL of isopropanol to a new 1.5mL LoBind tube and carefully add the supernatant from the previous step by pipetting from the air-liquid interface the sample, mix by inverting the tube 50 times

Discard the supernatant by slowly drawing with a pipette at the air-liquid interface to avoid disturbing the pellet, which may be difficult to see Pellets when precipitated with isopropanol will be near invisible, and easily detached from the tube, so care must be taken not to discard the DNA pellet.
Add 300 µL of 70 % (v/v) ethanol to the sample and invert 10 times to wash the pellet
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
Discard the supernatant, and allow the pellet to air dry for 00:05:00
Add 30 µL of DNA hydration solution or nuclease free water to the pellet
Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 to dissolve the DNA, or incubate in the fridge at 4 °C Overnight

After this step, DNA is ready to be quantified and used downstream. DNA resuspended in water should be stored at -20 °C , while DNA resuspended in DNA hydration solution can be stored for up to one month at 4 °C

Zinc chloride solution ( 2 Molarity (M) ) Weigh 0.68 g of ZnCl 2 and add to 2.5 mL of molecular grade water Carefully add 2.5 µL of 32 % (v/v) hydrochloric acid to the zinc solution and gently swirl to allow complete dissolution. Note : this step generates heat Measure a small aliquot of the solution on a pH meter, a 2 Molarity (M) solution of zinc chloride should have a pH pH 4.5 to pH 7 to ensure Zn +2 ions are available in solution
EDTA Solution ( 0.1 Molarity (M) ) Add 200 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) molecular grade EDTA to 800 µL of UltraPure water
Centrifuge for 00:10:00 at 8000 rcf, Room temperature, and remove supernatant by aspiration

Add 50 µL of 0.1 Molarity (M) EDTA solution to the phage pellet, pipetting and stirring gently with the pipette tip to break up the pellet, noting it is not necessary to resuspend the pellet during this step
The precipitated proteins should form a tight pellet. If the protein pellet is not tight, or there is still some in solution, repeat the incubation and centrifugation
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Centrifuge at 16000 rcf, Room temperature, 5 minutes placing the hinge of the tube at the top of the centrifuge to assist in identifying the location of the DNA pellet once centrifuged
creator: Mitchell Hedges
